name: 群落
subject: biology
curriculum_points: 本模块包括遗传的细胞基础、遗传的分子基础、遗传的基本规律、生物的变异和生物的进化等内容。
通过进化形成物种多样性和适应性，进化的本质是遗传物质的改变。
选取的现代生物进化理论和物种形成等知识，主要是为了阐明生物进化的过程和原因。
textbook_chapter: 选择性必修二 第2章

port.
A4.2.7—Need for several approaches to conservation of biodiversity
No single approach by itself is sufficient, and different species require different measures. Include in situ
conservation of species in natural habitats, management of nature reserves, rewilding and reclamation of
degraded ecosystems, ex situ conservation in zoos and botanic gardens and storage of germ plasm in
seed or tissue banks.
A4.2.8—Selection of evolutionarily distinct and globally endangered species for conservatio
excerpts.reason: 链接问题提到变异对生态群落稳定性的贡献，与群落结构和功能相关。

excerpts.text:
Syllabus content
Standard level and higher level: 3 hours
Guiding questions
• How are the adaptations and habitats of species related?
• What causes the similarities between ecosystems within a terrestrial biome?
SL and HL
B4.1.1—Habitat as the place in which a community, species, population or organism lives
A description of the habitat of a species can include both geographical and physical locations, and the
type of ecosystem.
B4.1.2—Adaptations of organisms to the abiotic environment of thei
excerpts.reason: 提及栖息地是群落、物种等生存的场所，隐含群落概念。

excerpts.text:
Syllabus content
Application of skills: Students should examine models or digital collections of skulls to infer diet from the
anatomical features. Examples may include Homo sapiens (humans), Homo floresiensis and Paranthropus
robustus.
NOS: Deductions can be made from theories. In this example, observation of living mammals led to
theories relating dentition to herbivorous or carnivorous diets. These theories allowed the diet of extinct
organisms to be deduced.
B4.2.9—Adaptations of herbivores
excerpts.reason: 文本涉及捕食者与猎物、植物对光照的竞争等种间关系，属于群落生态学范畴。

Ecology and the Biosphere 44.1 The Scope of Ecology 44.2 Biogeography 44.3 Terrestrial Biomes 44.4 Aquatic Biomes 44.5 Climate and the Effects of Global Climate Change Why study ecology? Perhaps you are interested in learning about the natural world and how living things have adapted to the physical conditions of their environment. Or, perhaps you’re a future physician seeking to understand the connection between your patients' health and their environment. Humans are a part of the ecological landscape, and human health is one important part of human interaction with our physical and living environment. Lyme disease, for instance, serves as one modern-day example of the connection between our health and the natural world (Figure 44.1). More formally known as Lyme borreliosis, Lyme disease is a bacterial infection that can be transmitted to humans when they are bitten by the deer tick (Ixodes scapularis in the eastern U.S., and Ixodes pacificus along the Pacific coast). Deer ticks are the primary vectors (a vector is an organism that transmits a pathogen) for this disease. However, not all ticks carry the pathogen, and not all deer ticks carry the bacteria that will cause Lyme disease in humans. Also, the ticks I. scapularis and pacificus can have other hosts besides deer. In fact, it turns out that the probability of infection depends on the type of host upon which the tick develops: a higher proportion of ticks that live on white-footed mice carry the bacterium than do ticks that live on deer. Knowledge about the environments and population densities in which the host spec

This image appears on page 1324 of the textbook. ![Figure 1050 - A large illustration or diagram (450×800px) related to: FIGU](OpenStax_Biology_2e_images/img_1050_d53c6639.jpg) pheromones and a carbohydrate-rich substance that is an important energy source for the ants. Both the Karner blue larvae and the ants benefit from their interaction, although the species of attendant ants may be partially opportunistic and vary over the range of the butterfly. Ecosystem Ecology Ecosystem ecology is an extension of organismal, population, and community ecology. The ecosystem is composed of all the biotic components (living things) in an area along with the abiotic components (nonliving things) of that area. Some of the abiotic components include air, water, and soil. Ecosystem biologists ask questions about how nutrients and energy are stored and how they move among organisms and through the surrounding atmosphere, soil, and water. The Karner blue butterflies and the wild lupine live in an oak-pine barren habitat. This habitat is characterized by natural disturbance and nutrient-poor soils that are low in nitrogen. The availability of nutrients is an important factor in the distribution of the plants that live in this habitat. Researchers interested in ecosystem ecology could ask questions about the importance of limited resources and the movement of resources, such as nutrients, though the

supplements.curriculum_md_raw:
# 群落

## 基本信息
- **学科**: 生物学
- **学段**: high
- **年级**: 11
- **章节/领域**: 生态学
- **内容来源**: TeachAny 课标知识点卫星文件

## 一、课标原文

### 1.1 内容要求
> 本模块包括遗传的细胞基础、遗传的分子基础、遗传的基本规律、生物的变异和生物的进化等内容。
> 通过进化形成物种多样性和适应性，进化的本质是遗传物质的改变。
> 选取的现代生物进化理论和物种形成等知识，主要是为了阐明生物进化的过程和原因。

## 二、教学目标

- 理解「群落」的核心概念、方法与应用场景。
- 能结合材料、问题或真实情境解释「群落」相关现象。
- 能用规范的学科语言表达思路、依据和结论。

## 三、教学重难点

- 教学重点：建立对「群落」核心概念和基本方法的准确理解。
- 教学难点：把知识迁移到新的问题、材料或真实情境中。

## 五、教学建议

### 5.1 教学方法
- 以结构与功能、生命过程或生态关系为主线组织「群落」教学。
- 通过图示、模型、实验或真实案例，引导学生解释生命现象背后的机制。
- 围绕课标要点“本模块包括遗传的细胞基础、遗传的分子基础、遗传的基本规律、生物的变异和生物的进化等内容。”，设计观察记录、证据解释和生活应用任务。

### 5.2 学习活动设计
1. 以结构与功能、生命过程或生态关系为主线组织「群落」教学。
2. 通过图示、模型、实验或真实案例，引导学生解释生命现象背后的机制。
3. 围绕课标要点“本模块包括遗传的细胞基础、遗传的分子基础、遗传的基本规律、生物的变异和生物的进化等内容。”，设计观察记录、证据解释和生活应用任务。

## 七、课件制作参考

- 以结构与功能、生命过程或生态关系为主线组织「群落」教学。
- 通过图示、模型、实验或真实案例，引导学生解释生命现象背后的机制。
- 围绕课标要点“本模块包括遗传的细胞基础、遗传的分子基础、遗传的基本规律、生物的变异和生物的进化等内容。”，设计观察记录、证据解释和生活应用任务。

## 八、相关知识扩展

- 联系健康、生态、农业或生物技术案例，讨论「群落」与现实生活的关系。
- 通过模型图、显微图或调查资料拓展学生对生命系统的理解。
supplements.textbook_summary:
## 教材化讲解参考（由课标要点生成）

### 核心概念
群落是生物学学习中的重要知识点。教学时应围绕课标要点“本模块包括遗传的细胞基础、遗传的分子基础、遗传的基本规律、生物的变异和生物的进化等内容。”展开，先明确概念边界，再通过例子、图示、实验、语篇或案例帮助学生建立可迁移的理解。

### 学习路径
1. 情境导入：以结构与功能、生命过程或生态关系为主线组织「群落」教学。
2. 探究建构：通过图示、模型、实验或真实案例，引导学生解释生命现象背后的机制。
3. 应用迁移：围绕课标要点“本模块包括遗传的细胞基础、遗传的分子基础、遗传的基本规律、生物的变异和生物的进化等内容。”，设计观察记录、证据解释和生活应用任务。

### 例题设计方向
可设计一道“识别核心概念”的基础题，一道“解释现象或文本材料”的理解题，一道“迁移到真实情境”的应用题，并要求学生说明依据。

errors.description: 把名词当结论，不结合结构与功能
忽视实验证据与变量控制

exercises.stem: 依据课标「群落」：本模块包括遗传的细胞基础、遗传的分子基础、遗传的基本规律、生物的变异和生物的进化等内容。 请简要阐述并举例。
exercises.answer: 应答覆盖课标要点，体现掌握与应用。

exercises.stem: 比较说明：通过进化形成物种多样性和适应性，进化的本质是遗传物质的改变。
exercises.answer: 需写出关键概念、过程或方法，避免只背结论。

real_world: 观察人体健康、植物生长、生态环境或食品生产中与「群落」相关的生命现象。
